austin.title: Austin
austin.tagline: Live Music Capital of the World
austin.gettingThere: Fly into Austin-Bergstrom International Airport (AUS), 8 miles southeast of downtown â€” about 20 minutes by Uber ($20-30) or 40 minutes by MetroExpress bus ($1.25). Southwest, American, United, Delta, and Frontier all serve AUS with non-stop flights from most major US cities.
highlights: 6th Street entertainment district â€” 60+ live music venues in six blocks
South Congress Avenue (SoCo) â€” boutiques, food trucks, and the Continental Club
Barton Springs Pool â€” spring-fed natural swimming hole in Zilker Park ($5-9)
Texas State Capitol â€” free tours of the 1888 pink granite landmark
Franklin Barbecue â€” legendary Central Texas brisket, arrive before 9am ($25-35/lb)
Lady Bird Lake hike-and-bike trail â€” 10-mile loop through downtown
The Blanton Museum of Art on UT campus (free Thursdays)
Rainey Street bar district â€” converted bungalows with craft beer and live music

essentials.gettingAround: Car rental or rideshare essential outside downtown. Capital Metro bus and rail covers central Austin. Lime and Bird scooters available. Downtown is walkable.
essentials.airport: AUS â€” Austin-Bergstrom International Airport

houston.title: Houston
houston.tagline: Space City â€” America's Most Diverse Metropolis
houston.gettingThere: George Bush Intercontinental (IAH) is 23 miles north of downtown â€” the main international hub. William P. Hobby Airport (HOU) is 7 miles southeast and handles Southwest flights plus some internationals. IAH to downtown by SuperShuttle runs $25; Uber/Lyft $35-55.
highlights: Space Center Houston â€” NASA's official visitor center with Saturn V rocket ($34)
Houston Museum District â€” 19 museums including the free Menil Collection
Buffalo Bayou Park â€” 160-acre green corridor with skyline views and kayaking
The Galleria â€” 400 stores, two hotels, and an ice rink in one mall
Hermann Park and Houston Zoo ($25)
Montrose and Midtown â€” Houston's eclectic dining and LGBTQ+ hub
Kemah Boardwalk amusement park on Galveston Bay (40 min south)
Houston Livestock Show & Rodeo â€” world's largest, 20 days in February-March

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential â€” Houston is spread across 670 sq miles. METRORail light rail serves the Museum District and Midtown. Uber/Lyft widely available. Parking is cheap and plentiful.
essentials.airport: IAH â€” George Bush Intercontinental / HOU â€” William P. Hobby Airport

san-antonio.title: San Antonio
san-antonio.gettingThere: Fly into San Antonio International Airport (SAT), 8 miles north of downtown. Uber/Lyft to downtown costs $18-28 and takes 20 minutes. American, Southwest, United, and Delta serve SAT. Amtrak's Sunset Limited stops in San Antonio three times weekly from Los Angeles and New Orleans.
highlights: The Alamo â€” the Shrine of Texas Liberty, free admission
San Antonio River Walk (Paseo del Rio) â€” 15-mile network of stone paths, restaurants, and river barges
Pearl District â€” converted Pearl Brewery with 50+ restaurants, a farmers market, and Hotel Emma
San Antonio Missions World Heritage Site â€” four 18th-century Spanish colonial missions
La Villita Historic Arts Village â€” oldest neighborhood in the city
San Fernando Cathedral â€” Texas's oldest active cathedral
Market Square (El Mercado) â€” largest Mexican market in the US
Tower of the Americas 750-foot observation deck ($14)

essentials.gettingAround: VIA streetcar and bus serve downtown. The River Walk connects major attractions on foot. Uber/Lyft for wider explorations. Bike share available along the river.
essentials.airport: SAT â€” San Antonio International Airport

dallas.title: Dallas
dallas.tagline: Big D â€” Where Ambition Meets Texas Style
dallas.gettingThere: Dallas/Fort Worth International (DFW) is one of the world's busiest airports, served by virtually every major airline. DART Orange Line connects DFW to downtown Dallas in 45 minutes ($2.50). Dallas Love Field (DAL) is 6 miles northwest â€” Southwest's main hub, 15 minutes to downtown by Uber ($15-20).
highlights: Sixth Floor Museum at Dealey Plaza â€” definitive JFK assassination museum ($18)
Dallas Arts District â€” largest urban arts district in the US with AT&T Performing Arts Center
Deep Ellum â€” live music, murals, craft breweries, and BBQ
Reunion Tower GeO-Deck â€” 360-degree views from 470 feet ($20)
Dallas Arboretum and Botanical Garden on White Rock Lake ($17)
Perot Museum of Nature and Science (free Tuesdays, otherwise $25)
Bishop Arts District â€” 60+ locally-owned shops and restaurants
NorthPark Center â€” Dallas's landmark upscale mall with rotating art exhibitions

essentials.gettingAround: DART light rail covers major areas including Uptown, Downtown, and Bishop Arts. Car recommended for wider Dallas. Uber/Lyft active. Downtown is walkable.
essentials.airport: DFW â€” Dallas/Fort Worth International / DAL â€” Dallas Love Field

fort-worth.title: Fort Worth
fort-worth.gettingThere: Fly into Dallas/Fort Worth International (DFW), 17 miles northeast â€” TRE commuter rail to downtown Fort Worth in 35 minutes ($5.50). Or fly into Dallas Love Field and drive 35 miles west via I-30. Amtrak's Texas Eagle stops at Fort Worth's historic T&P Station weekly.
highlights: Fort Worth Stockyards National Historic District â€” twice-daily longhorn cattle drive on Exchange Avenue
Kimbell Art Museum â€” Louis Kahn masterpiece with Caravaggio, Michelangelo, and Rembrandt (free)
Amon Carter Museum of American Art (free)
Sundance Square â€” 35-block walkable entertainment district
Fort Worth Botanic Garden â€” Texas's oldest botanical garden (free grounds)
Bass Performance Hall â€” world-class performing arts venue
National Cowgirl Museum and Hall of Fame ($15)
Billy Bob's Texas â€” world's largest honky-tonk with indoor rodeo

essentials.gettingAround: Molly the Trolley free downtown shuttle. Uber/Lyft for stockyards and museums. Car recommended for wider Fort Worth. TRE rail to/from DFW airport.
essentials.airport: DFW â€” Dallas/Fort Worth International Airport

big-bend.title: Big Bend National Park
big-bend.tagline: Texas's Last Frontier â€” Desert Canyons and Dark Skies
big-bend.gettingThere: Big Bend is deliberately remote. Drive from El Paso (4.5 hours via I-10 and US-90) or Midland-Odessa (3.5 hours via US-385). Fly to Midland International (MAF) and rent a car. The nearest gas is in Study Butte or Marathon â€” fill up before entering the park. Park entry is $35/vehicle, valid 7 days.
highlights: Santa Elena Canyon Trail â€” 1.7-mile out-and-back into sheer 1,500-foot canyon walls
Rio Grande Hot Springs â€” soaking pools on the river at 105Â°F (5-mile round trip)
Bortle 1 dark sky stargazing â€” among the darkest skies in the continental US
Chisos Basin and Window Trail with mountain lion habitat
Ross Maxwell Scenic Drive â€” 30 miles of volcanic formations and desert vistas
Boquillas del Carmen border crossing to Mexico by rowboat ($5)
Emory Peak â€” Big Bend's highest point at 7,832 feet (8.5 miles round trip)
Lost Mine Trail panoramic ridge hike at sunrise

essentials.gettingAround: Personal vehicle essential â€” no public transport inside the park. High-clearance 4WD recommended for dirt roads. Gas available at Panther Junction Visitor Center. Cell service essentially nonexistent.
essentials.airport: MAF â€” Midland International Air and Space Port (3.5 hours away)

fredericksburg.title: Fredericksburg
fredericksburg.tagline: Texas Wine Country and German Heritage
fredericksburg.gettingThere: Drive from Austin (1 hour 20 minutes via US-290 West) or San Antonio (1 hour 10 minutes via I-10 West to TX-16 North). No commercial airport â€” nearest is San Antonio (SAT) or Austin (AUS). US-290 through the Hill Country is one of Texas's most scenic drives.
highlights: Texas Wine Trail on US-290 â€” 50+ Hill Country wineries within 30 miles
Enchanted Rock State Natural Area â€” massive pink granite dome with summit hike ($8/person)
National Museum of the Pacific War (Admiral Nimitz Museum) â€” world-class WWII museum ($18)
Main Street antiques, German bakeries, and boutique shopping
Wildseed Farms â€” largest working wildflower farm in the US (free entry)
Fredericksburg Brewing Company â€” Texas's oldest brewpub (1994)
Luckenbach Texas dance hall and live music every weekend
Grape Creek Vineyards estate tastings with Hill Country views ($15-25)

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Main Street walkable. Wine trail requires driving between wineries â€” use a designated driver or hire a wine tour shuttle. Bike rentals available in town.
essentials.airport: SAT â€” San Antonio International (70 min) / AUS â€” Austin-Bergstrom (80 min)

galveston.title: Galveston
galveston.tagline: Texas Beach Getaway â€” Victorian Island on the Gulf
galveston.gettingThere: Drive from Houston (60 minutes via I-45 South) â€” the most common approach. No commercial airport on the island; fly into Houston IAH or HOU then drive. Galveston is 50 miles south of Houston. The 2-mile causeway onto the island can back up on summer weekends.
highlights: The Strand Historic District â€” Victorian iron-front buildings with galleries, restaurants, and Mardi Gras festivities
Moody Gardens â€” three glass pyramids with aquarium, rainforest, and IMAX ($30-65 combo)
Stewart Beach and East Beach â€” family and party beaches with facilities
Galveston Island State Park â€” unspoiled beach and birding on the west end ($5/person)
Pleasure Pier amusement park on the Gulf with the Iron Shark coaster ($35)
Galveston Railroad Museum â€” restored 1930s Santa Fe depot
Schlitterbahn Galveston water park (summer only, $60)
Fresh Gulf seafood â€” shrimp, redfish, and blue crab at Fisherman's Wharf or Gaido's

essentials.gettingAround: Car recommended. The Island Transit bus ($1.25) covers main routes. Seawall Boulevard is bikeable. Golf cart rentals popular on the west end. Ride-share available but limited.
essentials.airport: HOU â€” William P. Hobby Airport, Houston (60 min drive)

marfa.title: Marfa
marfa.tagline: Art Meets Desert â€” The World's Most Unlikely Art Town
marfa.gettingThere: Drive from El Paso (3 hours east via I-10 to US-90) or Midland/Odessa (2.5 hours southwest via US-385). No commercial service at the small local airport. The drive through alpine high desert on US-90 and TX-67 is spectacular â€” Marfa sits at 4,688 feet elevation.
highlights: Chinati Foundation â€” Donald Judd's monumental art installations in converted military buildings ($30)
Marfa Mystery Lights â€” unexplained atmospheric phenomenon viewed from the official viewing area east of town
Ballroom Marfa gallery and cultural space (free)
Prada Marfa â€” Elmgreen and Dragset's permanent art installation on US-90 near Valentine
Hotel Saint George rooftop bar with desert panoramas
The Do Your Thang food truck scene on Highland Avenue
Alpine (30 min east) and Presidio (60 min south) day trips
Fort Davis National Historic Site and McDonald Observatory (45 min north, star parties from $20)

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Marfa itself is walkable (population 1,800). No rideshare â€” rent a car from El Paso or Midland. Fill up with gas before arriving; supplies are limited.
essentials.airport: ELP â€” El Paso International Airport (3 hours west)

corpus-christi.title: Corpus Christi
corpus-christi.gettingThere: Fly into Corpus Christi International Airport (CRP) with connections via American and United through Dallas and Houston hubs. Or drive from San Antonio (2.5 hours via I-37 South) or Houston (3.5 hours via US-59 South). CRP is 10 minutes from downtown.
highlights: USS Lexington Museum â€” decommissioned WWII aircraft carrier open for self-guided tours ($18)
Texas State Aquarium â€” Gulf ecosystems with dolphins, sharks, and sea turtles ($25)
Padre Island National Seashore â€” longest stretch of undeveloped barrier island in the US (free with park pass)
Corpus Christi Museum of Science and History ($8)
The Harbor Bridge waterfront and Whataburger Field minor league ballpark
North Beach â€” calm bay-side beach popular with families
Port Aransas â€” 30-minute ferry ride to the surf beach town (ferry is free)
Mustang Island State Park camping and surf fishing ($5/person)

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential for Padre Island and beyond. Downtown and the bayfront are walkable. Regional Transit Authority (RTA) bus covers main corridors. Free ferry to Port Aransas from the north end of Mustang Island.
essentials.airport: CRP â€” Corpus Christi International Airport

el-paso.title: El Paso
el-paso.gettingThere: Fly into El Paso International Airport (ELP), 5 miles northeast of downtown. American, Southwest, United, and Frontier serve ELP with connections through Dallas, Houston, Phoenix, and Denver. Uber to downtown costs $15-20. Sun Metro city bus #33 runs from the airport.
highlights: Franklin Mountains State Park â€” 26,000 acres of desert hiking inside city limits, accessible by aerial tramway ($4.50)
El Paso Mission Trail â€” three 17th-century Spanish missions (Ysleta, Socorro, San Elizario) east of the city
El Paso Museum of Art downtown (free)
Sunland Park Racetrack and Casino just across the New Mexico border
Ciudad JuÃ¡rez day trip across the border â€” markets, murals, and street tacos (bring US passport)
Concordia Cemetery â€” historic resting place of gunslingers and Buffalo Soldiers
Hueco Tanks State Park and Historic Site â€” rock climbing and ancient Native American pictographs ($7)
Downtown El Paso walkable arts district with murals and local restaurants

essentials.gettingAround: Car recommended for the sprawling city. Sun Metro bus covers main routes. Downtown walkable. El Paso Streetcar (historic) runs through downtown and the University District ($1.50).
essentials.airport: ELP â€” El Paso International Airport

south-padre-island.title: South Padre Island
south-padre-island.tagline: Tropical Texas Paradise on the Gulf
south-padre-island.gettingThere: Fly into Valley International Airport (HRL) in Harlingen â€” 30 minutes from the island via US-83 and Queen Isabella Causeway. American and Southwest serve HRL. Brownsville South Padre Island International (BRO) is another option, 45 minutes away. SPI is the southernmost tip of Texas, 5 miles from the Mexican border.
highlights: South Padre Island beach â€” wide Gulf shoreline with warm water and gentle waves
Sea Turtle Inc. rehabilitation centre (free, but donations welcomed)
Dolphin and whale watching tours in the Laguna Madre (from $25)
Andy Bowie Park â€” unspoiled north island with camping and birding
Schlitterbahn South Padre Island water park (summer, $50)
SandCastle Days â€” US Open Sand Castle Competition in October
Wakeboarding and kiteboarding lessons on the bay side (from $60)
Night fishing and crabbing on the causeway bridge

essentials.gettingAround: Car required to get on and off the island. The island itself is walkable or bikeable north-south along Padre Blvd. Wave (trolley) bus runs the length of the island in summer ($2). Uber limited.
essentials.airport: HRL â€” Valley International Airport, Harlingen (30 min drive)

waco.title: Waco
waco.gettingThere: Drive from Dallas (1.5 hours south on I-35) or Austin (1.5 hours north on I-35). No major commercial airport â€” nearest are Dallas/Fort Worth (DFW) and Austin-Bergstrom (AUS), both 90 miles away. Waco is perfectly positioned as a day trip or overnight stop between the two cities.
highlights: Magnolia Market at the Silos â€” Chip and Joanna Gaines's flagship shop, gardens, and food trucks
Dr Pepper Museum â€” birthplace of Dr Pepper in 1885 (free entry, tastings $5)
Waco Mammoth National Monument â€” fossil site of Columbian mammoths ($8)
Cameron Park Zoo with 1,700 animals ($13)
Baylor University campus and the Bear Habitat (free)
Lake Waco waterfront parks and paddleboarding
Balcones Distilling â€” award-winning Texas whisky tasting room
Texas Ranger Hall of Fame and Museum ($7)

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Downtown Waco is walkable around the Magnolia Silos area. No meaningful public transit. Uber/Lyft available but limited outside peak hours.
essentials.airport: DFW â€” Dallas/Fort Worth International (90 min north) / AUS â€” Austin-Bergstrom (90 min south)

new-braunfels.title: New Braunfels
new-braunfels.tagline: Texas Tubing Capital and German Roots
new-braunfels.gettingThere: Drive from San Antonio (30 minutes north on I-35) or Austin (45 minutes south on I-35). Perfectly situated between the two cities. No commercial airport â€” use SAT or AUS. The Comal and Guadalupe rivers run through town and are the main attractions.
highlights: Schlitterbahn New Braunfels â€” consistently rated America's best water park ($60-80)
Tubing the Comal River â€” 2.5-mile natural waterway through downtown ($20-30 with tube rental)
Tubing the Guadalupe River â€” class I-II rapids, 5-7 mile float trips
Gruene Hall â€” Texas's oldest dance hall (1878) with live music every weekend
Historic Gruene district shops and restaurants along the river
Natural Bridge Caverns â€” one of Texas's most spectacular cave systems ($28)
Landa Park with Comal Springs â€” largest springs in Texas
Wurstfest â€” 10-day German sausage and beer festival in November

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Historic Gruene and downtown New Braunfels are walkable. River operators provide shuttle buses between put-in and take-out points. No public transit.
essentials.airport: SAT â€” San Antonio International (30 min south) / AUS â€” Austin-Bergstrom (45 min north)

san-marcos.title: San Marcos
san-marcos.tagline: Crystal Springs and College Town Energy
san-marcos.gettingThere: Drive from Austin (30 minutes south on I-35) or San Antonio (50 minutes north on I-35). No commercial airport. Conveniently located at the midpoint of the Austin-San Antonio corridor. Greyhound and Flixbus stop at San Marcos on their Austin-San Antonio runs.
highlights: Aquarena Springs â€” glass-bottom boats over the crystal-clear San Marcos Springs ($9)
Tubing the San Marcos River through City Park ($5 tube rental)
Wonder World Cave and Earthquake Fault Tour â€” unique tectonics geology ($27)
San Marcos Premium Outlets â€” two malls with 150+ outlet stores
Wimberley Blue Hole Regional Park (day pass, reservation required, $10)
Texas State University campus and the LBJ Student Center
Downtown square bars and music venues for Texas State students and visitors
Tangerine restaurant for Hill Country farm-to-table dining

essentials.gettingAround: Car recommended. Downtown square and riverside are walkable. CARTS rural transit connects to surrounding Hill Country towns. Bike-friendly with greenway trails.
essentials.airport: AUS â€” Austin-Bergstrom (30 min north) / SAT â€” San Antonio International (50 min south)

wimberley.title: Wimberley
wimberley.tagline: Hill Country Artists' Village on the Blanco River
wimberley.gettingThere: Drive from Austin (45 minutes southwest via TX-290 W and TX-12) or San Antonio (1 hour via I-35 N and TX-12). No public transit. RV Road is TX-12 through the Hill Country â€” allow time for the scenic drive through Wimberley Valley.
highlights: Blue Hole Regional Park â€” famous spring-fed swimming hole on the Blanco River ($10, advance reservation required)
Wimberley Zipline Adventures over the Blanco River canyon
Market Days at Lions Field â€” huge outdoor market on the first Saturday of each month (Mar-Dec)
EmilyAnn Theatre outdoor amphitheatre performances
Cypress Creek Nature Trail boardwalk through spring-fed stream
Wimberley Glass Works â€” working studio and gallery
The Driftwood Estate Winery (25 min east)
Millrace Park for paddleboarding and kayaking on the Blanco

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Village square is walkable. No public transit or rideshare. Book Blue Hole reservations weeks in advance in summer.
essentials.airport: AUS â€” Austin-Bergstrom (45 min northeast)

dripping-springs.title: Dripping Springs
dripping-springs.tagline: Wedding Capital of Texas and Hill Country Spirits
dripping-springs.gettingThere: Drive from Austin (30 minutes west on US-290) or Fredericksburg (45 minutes east on US-290). The US-290 corridor through Dripping Springs is Texas's Distillery District â€” home to over a dozen craft spirits producers. No public transit.
highlights: Dripping Springs Distillery District â€” Treaty Oak, Desert Door, Bendt, and a dozen more on US-290
Hamilton Pool Preserve â€” stunning collapsed grotto with 50-foot waterfall ($30, reservation required)
Pedernales Falls State Park â€” swimming holes and limestone waterfalls ($6)
Jester King Brewery â€” award-winning farmhouse ales in a 165-acre ranch setting
Salt Lick BBQ in Driftwood â€” legendary pit BBQ on a cash-only hilltop ranch
Mercer Street district boutiques and farm-to-table restaurants
Firefly Resort glamping with Hill Country views
Reimers Ranch Park on the Pedernales for rock climbing and swimming

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Designate a driver for the distillery trail. Hamilton Pool requires advance reservation â€” book online weeks ahead in spring. No rideshare in the rural areas.
essentials.airport: AUS â€” Austin-Bergstrom (30 min east)

round-rock.title: Round Rock
round-rock.tagline: Dell Diamond, Donuts, and Outfield Sports
round-rock.gettingThere: Drive from Austin (20 minutes north on I-35). Part of the greater Austin metropolitan area. No separate commercial airport â€” use AUS. Capital Metro rail (MetroRail) connects downtown Austin to downtown Round Rock ($3.50).
highlights: Round Rock Express AAA baseball at Dell Diamond â€” minor league baseball from $12
Round Rock Donuts â€” famous Texas-sized donuts, served since 1926
Old Settlers Park and sports complex
Round Rock Premium Outlets â€” 125+ outlet stores
Rock'N River Family Aquatic Center (summer, $10)
Brushy Creek Lake Park trails and fishing
Round Rock historic downtown square
IKEA Round Rock â€” Texas's largest home goods destination for shoppers

essentials.gettingAround: Car recommended. MetroRail to Austin. Downtown walkable. Uber/Lyft active.
essentials.airport: AUS â€” Austin-Bergstrom International Airport (20 min south)

georgetown.title: Georgetown
georgetown.tagline: The Most Beautiful Town Square in Texas
georgetown.gettingThere: Drive from Austin (30 minutes north on I-35). Capital Metro MetroRail commuter train connects Georgetown to downtown Austin in 60 minutes ($4.50). MetroRail is a convenient car-free option from Austin.
highlights: Georgetown historic downtown square â€” Williamson County Courthouse (1911) and locally-owned shops
Blue Hole Park on the South Fork of the San Gabriel River (free)
Georgetown's Inner Space Cavern â€” active cave system open for guided tours ($20)
Lake Georgetown reservoir for boating, fishing, and camping
Southwestern University campus â€” Texas's oldest university (founded 1840)
Wildflower season (March-April) along roadsides and Georgetown's parks
Russell Fish hatchery and visitor center (free)
Two Steps Wine Trail on the nearby Hill Country

essentials.gettingAround: MetroRail from Austin to Georgetown station. Car recommended once in Georgetown. Downtown walkable. CARTS bus connects surrounding communities.
essentials.airport: AUS â€” Austin-Bergstrom International Airport (35 min south)

marble-falls.title: Marble Falls
marble-falls.gettingThere: Drive from Austin (50 minutes northwest via US-183 to US-281) or San Antonio (1.5 hours via I-35 N and TX-71 W). Part of the Highland Lakes chain formed by six dams on the Colorado River. No public transit.
highlights: Lake Marble Falls and Lake LBJ â€” fishing, boating, and lakeside restaurants
Bluebonnet Trail drives in spring (March-April) through the Hill Country
Balcones Canyonlands National Wildlife Refuge birding (free)
Longhorn Cavern State Park â€” 11-mile cave tour ($20)
Inks Lake State Park â€” swimming, paddling, and camping on crystal-clear water ($6)
Falls Creek Winery tasting room overlooking the lake
Bluebonnet Cafe â€” legendary small-town diner since 1929 (pie is mandatory)
Colorado Bend State Park tubing and gorman falls hiking

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Small walkable downtown. No public transit or rideshare. Boat rentals available on the lakefront.
essentials.airport: AUS â€” Austin-Bergstrom International Airport (50 min east)

kerrville.title: Kerrville
kerrville.tagline: Heart of the Texas Hill Country
kerrville.gettingThere: Drive from San Antonio (1 hour northwest via I-10 W and TX-16 N) or Austin (1.5 hours via TX-71 W). No commercial airport â€” use SAT or AUS. The Guadalupe River runs through Kerrville and is the focal point of outdoor recreation.
highlights: Guadalupe River State Park tubing and swimming ($7)
Kerrville Folk Festival â€” 18-day acoustic music extravaganza in May-June
Museum of Western Art with cowboy and ranching heritage ($5)
Louise Hays Park on the Guadalupe River (free)
Cowboy Capital of the World Rodeo and Western heritage events
Schreiner University campus and grounds
Y.O. Ranch Day Trips for Texas longhorn and exotic game
Hill Country Arts Foundation theater and gallery

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Downtown Kerrville walkable along the river. No public transit. River tube rentals downtown in summer.
essentials.airport: SAT â€” San Antonio International (60 min southeast)

boerne.title: Boerne
boerne.gettingThere: Drive from San Antonio (30 minutes northwest via I-10 W). A popular weekend escape from San Antonio. No commercial airport â€” use SAT. Exit 543 on I-10 leads straight to historic Main Street.
highlights: Boerne historic Main Street â€” antique shops, boutiques, and German restaurants
Cave Without a Name â€” stalactite and stalagmite show cave ($20)
Cibolo Nature Center on Cibolo Creek (free)
Cascade Caverns â€” family-friendly cave tour with underground waterfall ($22)
Herff Farm â€” local agricultural history and wildflower meadows
Boerne City Lake Park trails and fishing
Dienger Trading Company farm-to-table restaurant on Main Street
The Barrier Reef at Boerne Lake for swimming

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Main Street walkable. No public transit. Short drive to many Hill Country attractions.
essentials.airport: SAT â€” San Antonio International Airport (30 min southeast)

bandera.title: Bandera
bandera.gettingThere: Drive from San Antonio (45 minutes northwest via TX-16 N). The Medina River and Hill Country setting make Bandera a quintessential Texas cowboy experience. No commercial airport â€” use SAT.
highlights: Frontier Times Museum â€” Tex-Mex curios and frontier history ($5)
Medina River tubing and swimming in summer
Eleventh Street Cowboy Bar â€” honky-tonk with live country music nightly
Arkey Blue's Silver Dollar â€” oldest dance hall in Bandera
Bandera Downs racetrack on race days
Y.O. Ranch and Hill Country guest ranch stays (from $150/night all-inclusive)
Polly's Pit Stop BBQ â€” pit-smoked brisket in a converted gas station
Bandera Crossing â€” Medina River swimming hole

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Downtown Bandera walkable. No public transit or rideshare. Guest ranch stays typically include activities and transport.
essentials.airport: SAT â€” San Antonio International Airport (45 min southeast)

johnson-city.title: Johnson City
johnson-city.tagline: LBJ Country and Pedernales Wine Territory
johnson-city.gettingThere: Drive from Austin (50 minutes west via US-290 W) or Fredericksburg (30 minutes east via US-290 E). The US-290 Wine Corridor connects Johnson City to the Hill Country wine trail. No commercial airport.
highlights: Lyndon B. Johnson National Historical Park â€” LBJ's childhood home and Texas White House Ranch (free)
Pedernales Falls State Park â€” dramatic limestone falls and river swimming ($6)
LBJ State Park and Historic Site adjacent to the national park (free)
Alamo Springs Cafe â€” remote Hill Country burger institution
US-290 wine trail access â€” Pedernales, Kuhlman Cellars, and others nearby
Christmas lights at Blanco and Marble Falls (November-January)
Johnson City historic town square and Courthouse
Exotic Wildlife Association land and ranches viewing from public roads

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Small walkable downtown. No public transit. Self-guided LBJ auto tour available from visitor center.
essentials.airport: AUS â€” Austin-Bergstrom International Airport (50 min east)

luckenbach.title: Luckenbach
luckenbach.gettingThere: Drive from Fredericksburg (10 minutes east via Luckenbach Road / FM 1376) or Austin (1.5 hours via US-290 W). Luckenbach is tiny â€” a dance hall, a bar, a blacksmith shop, and a post office. No commercial airport nearby; use AUS or SAT.
highlights: Luckenbach Texas Dance Hall â€” 1849 limestone building with live music every weekend
World's Fair Pickin' Party acoustic jams Sunday afternoons
Willie Nelson's 4th of July Picnic held here most years
Shiner Bock on tap at the general store bar ($5)
Waylon and Willie memorabilia throughout the property
Finger-picking guitar circles under the oak trees (impromptu, free)
Adjacent to the Fredericksburg wine trail on US-290
Photo op at the iconic Luckenbach post office

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential â€” Luckenbach has no services beyond the venue. 10 minutes from Fredericksburg. Designated driver mandatory on dance hall nights.
essentials.airport: AUS â€” Austin-Bergstrom International Airport (90 min northeast)

enchanted-rock.title: Enchanted Rock
enchanted-rock.tagline: Ancient Pink Granite Dome Rising from the Hill Country
enchanted-rock.gettingThere: Drive from Fredericksburg (18 miles north via TX-965) or Austin (1 hour 45 minutes via US-290 W to TX-965). Enchanted Rock State Natural Area is on FM 965 â€” the road dead-ends at the park. Entry is $8/person; reservations mandatory during peak seasons via Texas Parks and Wildlife website.
highlights: Summit Trail to the top of the dome â€” stunning 360-degree Hill Country panoramas (2.6 miles round trip)
Rock climbing on the north face and surrounding smaller domes
Vernal pools on the summit hosting rare salamanders and fairy shrimp
Echo Canyon slot canyon hike (4.5 miles)
Geology: 1 billion-year-old pink Llano Uplift granite
Turkey Peak Trail for solitude and wildflower meadows
Dark sky stargazing â€” minimal light pollution in this remote Hill Country valley
Wildlife: white-tailed deer, ring-tailed cats, and canyon wrens

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. No transit. Park capacity is strictly managed â€” book day passes online in advance for spring and fall weekends. No camping (day use only for non-campers).
essentials.airport: AUS â€” Austin-Bergstrom International Airport (2 hours east)

palo-duro-canyon.title: Palo Duro Canyon
palo-duro-canyon.gettingThere: Drive from Amarillo (25 miles southeast via TX-217). Fly into Rick Husband Amarillo International Airport (AMA) â€” American and Southwest serve AMA from Dallas and other hubs. The canyon is the second-largest canyon in the United States after the Grand Canyon.
highlights: Lighthouse Trail â€” Palo Duro's iconic 5.8-mile hike to a 310-foot stone pillar
TEXAS Outdoor Musical â€” spectacular outdoor amphitheatre drama performed June-August ($20-45)
Juniper Ravine and Rojo Grande mountain biking trails
Prairie Dog Town Fork of the Red River canyon floor drive
Rock climbing on the canyon walls (permit required)
Equestrian rides on the canyon floor from the park stables
Capital Peak viewpoint for sunrise photography
Mesquite camping in the canyon bottom

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. 16-mile scenic drive in the canyon. Mountain bikes rentable at the park. No public transit. Park entry $8/person.
essentials.airport: AMA â€” Rick Husband Amarillo International Airport (25 min northwest)

amarillo.title: Amarillo
amarillo.tagline: Where the Texas Panhandle Meets Route 66
amarillo.gettingThere: Fly into Rick Husband Amarillo International Airport (AMA), 9 miles east of downtown. American and Southwest operate direct service to Dallas, Denver, Houston, and Las Vegas. Historic Route 66 (Sixth Street) runs through downtown â€” Amarillo is a classic road trip stop.
highlights: Cadillac Ranch â€” 10 graffiti-covered Cadillacs half-buried nose-down on a ranch along I-40 (free)
Palo Duro Canyon State Park (25 miles SE) â€” Grand Canyon of Texas
Big Texan Steak Ranch â€” free 72-oz steak if you eat it all in one hour
Historic Route 66 / Sixth Street entertainment district
Amarillo Zoo in Thompson Park (free)
Panhandle-Plains Historical Museum in Canyon â€” largest history museum in Texas ($15)
Wonderland Amusement Park (summer, $30)
Tascosa ghost town ruins at Cal Farley's Boys Ranch west of town

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Downtown walkable around Sixth Street. CityBus covers main routes. Uber/Lyft available.
essentials.airport: AMA â€” Rick Husband Amarillo International Airport

lubbock.title: Lubbock
lubbock.tagline: Buddy Holly's Hometown on the South Plains
lubbock.gettingThere: Fly into Lubbock Preston Smith International Airport (LBB) â€” American and Southwest serve LBB with connections through Dallas and Denver. LBB is 5 miles north of downtown. Car rental at the airport recommended.
highlights: Buddy Holly Center â€” comprehensive museum and performing arts center ($10)
Buddy Holly's Walk of Fame statues on the Crickets Avenue
Texas Tech University Museum of Texas Tech and Moody Planetarium (free)
Lubbock Lake Landmark â€” archaeological site with 12,000 years of human history (free)
Llano Estacado Winery â€” Texas High Plains wine country touring (free tasting)
Mackenzie Park and Prairie Dog Town â€” free-roaming colony in the urban park
Caprock Canyons State Park (100 miles south) â€” Texas bison herd and red rock canyon
Blue Light Live music venue and McAdoo's Seafood Company

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential in this sprawling flat city. Citibus covers main corridors ($1.50). Texas Tech area walkable. Uber/Lyft available.
essentials.airport: LBB â€” Lubbock Preston Smith International Airport

midland-odessa.title: Midland-Odessa
midland-odessa.gettingThere: Fly into Midland International Air and Space Port (MAF), served by American, United, and Southwest with direct flights to Dallas, Houston, Phoenix, and Las Vegas. MAF is the gateway airport for Big Bend National Park (3.5 hours south) and West Texas road trips.
highlights: Permian Basin Petroleum Museum â€” world's leading oil industry museum ($12)
George W. Bush Childhood Home Museum in Midland ($10)
Museum of the Southwest and Centennial Science Museum (free)
Odessa Meteor Crater â€” second largest in the US (free)
Odessa Jackalopes hockey at Ector County Coliseum
Buddy Holly's first performance was in Lubbock, but the Permian Basin is the oil heartland
Big Spring State Park (60 miles northeast) for prairie dog watching and birding
Gateway to Big Bend National Park and Guadalupe Mountains

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential â€” the two cities sprawl across the flat Permian Basin. No meaningful public transit. Uber/Lyft available. Rent a car at MAF for regional exploration.
essentials.airport: MAF â€” Midland International Air and Space Port

guadalupe-mountains.title: Guadalupe Mountains National Park
guadalupe-mountains.tagline: Texas's Highest Peaks and Ancient Fossil Reef
guadalupe-mountains.gettingThere: Drive from El Paso (110 miles east via US-62/180) or Carlsbad Caverns (35 miles northeast via US-62/180). No public transit. The park sits on the Texas-New Mexico border. Fill up with gas in El Paso or Carlsbad before arriving â€” none available in the park. Entry is $15/vehicle.
highlights: Guadalupe Peak â€” highest point in Texas at 8,751 feet, 8.4-mile round trip hike
El Capitan â€” massive limestone escarpment visible from 50 miles away
McKittrick Canyon fall foliage (October-November) â€” Texas's best autumn color
Permian Reef Geology Trail â€” 280 million-year-old fossilized reef now exposed
Pine Springs Visitor Center and Butterfield Overland Mail site
Dog Canyon remote campground accessible from New Mexico side
Salt Basin Dunes day hike through white gypsum sand
Smith Spring oasis trail â€” natural water source in the desert

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential and mandatory. Backcountry hiking only â€” no paved roads within the park. High-clearance vehicle recommended for Dog Canyon route. Carlsbad Caverns (NM) is 35 miles north for combined trip.
essentials.airport: ELP â€” El Paso International Airport (110 miles west)

port-aransas.title: Port Aransas
port-aransas.tagline: Texas's Laid-Back Surf Town on Mustang Island
port-aransas.gettingThere: Drive from Corpus Christi (30 minutes) via the free ferry or TX-361 bridge (free). Fly into Corpus Christi International (CRP), then drive. The free Aransas Pass ferry from the mainland is part of the Port A experience â€” short queues most of the year.
highlights: Mustang Island State Park beach â€” 5 miles of undeveloped Gulf shoreline ($5/person)
Deep sea and bay fishing charters (from $60/person half day)
Whooping crane viewing tours in winter at Aransas National Wildlife Refuge (from $45)
Roberts Point Park and fishing pier (free)
Port Aransas Birding Center (free) â€” exceptional shorebird and warbler habitat
Lydia Ann Channel dolphin boat tours
Beachcombing for sand dollars on the Gulf side
Tourist District bars, beach bars, and fresh Gulf seafood

essentials.gettingAround: Car recommended. The town is bikeable. Tram service runs along the main strip in peak season ($2). Free ferry from Aransas Pass to Port A. Uber/Lyft limited.
essentials.airport: CRP â€” Corpus Christi International Airport (45 min by car and ferry)

rockport-fulton.title: Rockport-Fulton
rockport-fulton.gettingThere: Drive from Corpus Christi (35 miles north via TX-35) or San Antonio (2.5 hours via US-181 and TX-35). No commercial airport â€” use CRP. The twin communities sit on Aransas Bay, sheltered from the Gulf.
highlights: Aransas National Wildlife Refuge â€” only wild flock of endangered whooping cranes winters here (Nov-Mar, $5/car)
Rockport Beach Park â€” one of Texas's best-kept beach secrets ($6/person)
Rockport Center for the Arts galleries (free)
Texas Maritime Museum covering 500 years of Gulf history ($8)
Hummingbird Festival in September â€” millions of ruby-throated hummingbirds migrate through
Rockport Sea Center Texas fish hatchery and aquarium (free)
Fulton Mansion State Historic Site â€” 1877 French Second Empire mansion ($5)
Kayaking the back bays for dolphin spotting

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Rockport beach and downtown walkable. No public transit. Kayak rentals on the bay.
essentials.airport: CRP â€” Corpus Christi International Airport (35 min south)

matagorda.title: Matagorda
matagorda.tagline: Unspoiled Texas Gulf Coast Fishing Village
matagorda.gettingThere: Drive from Houston (2 hours via TX-35 S and TX-60). Very remote â€” no commercial airport nearby. HOU (Houston Hobby) is the nearest at 85 miles. The drive down TX-35 and TX-60 along Matagorda Bay is worth it for the scenery.
highlights: Matagorda Beach â€” 35 miles of pristine undeveloped Gulf shoreline with no crowds
Matagorda Bay Nature Park â€” excellent birding and wildlife viewing (free)
Colorado River Lighthouse historic site
Fishing for redfish, flounder, and speckled trout in the bay
Matagorda Island State Park â€” only accessible by boat, truly undeveloped barrier island
Birding along the Great Texas Coastal Birding Trail
Shrimping boats and fresh catch at the local docks
Dark sky beach camping

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential and required. Very limited services in Matagorda â€” fill up gas in Bay City (30 min north). No rideshare or public transit.
essentials.airport: HOU â€” William P. Hobby Airport, Houston (85 miles north)

surfside-beach.title: Surfside Beach
surfside-beach.gettingThere: Drive from Houston (1 hour 20 minutes via TX-288 S to TX-332). No commercial airport. HOU (Houston Hobby) is the nearest at 55 miles. The Freeport Brazosport area connects to Surfside Beach on the western end of the Brazosport beaches.
highlights: Surfside Beach â€” casual Gulf beach popular with Houston day-trippers
Bryan Beach State Park adjacent to the town (free)
Fishing from the Surfside Jetties on the Freeport Ship Channel
Brazosport Museum of Natural Science in Lake Jackson ($5)
Quintana Beach County Park bird sanctuary and beach camping ($20/night)
Sea Center Texas in Lake Jackson â€” fish hatchery and aquarium (free)
Fresh shrimp off the boat at Freeport docks
Birding the Brazoria National Wildlife Refuge (free)

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Surfside Beach itself is a narrow barrier island â€” bikeable along the shore road. No public transit or rideshare.
essentials.airport: HOU â€” William P. Hobby Airport, Houston (55 miles north)

terlingua.title: Terlingua
terlingua.tagline: Ghost Town at the Edge of Big Bend
terlingua.gettingThere: Drive from Alpine (80 miles south via TX-118) or from Big Bend National Park's western entrance (8 miles). Midland (MAF) is the nearest commercial airport, 3.5 hours north. Terlingua serves as the basecamp for Big Bend visitors â€” book lodging months in advance for prime dates.
highlights: Terlingua Ghost Town cemetery and ruins â€” remnants of the quicksilver mining boomtown
Starlight Theatre restaurant and bar â€” legendary outdoor live music venue in the ruins
World Championship Chili Cook-Off â€” held first Saturday of November since 1967
Big Bend Ranch State Park (immediately adjacent) â€” more remote than the national park
Santa Elena Canyon raft trip launching from Terlingua ($100-200/day with guide)
Rio Grande Village and Boquillas hot springs day hike from Terlingua
Chili Pepper Cafe and La Kiva underground bar
Dark sky viewing â€” some of the darkest skies in the contiguous US

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential and mandatory. Terlingua has one road in and one road out. Stock up on supplies in Alpine before driving down. Gas available but limited and expensive in Terlingua.
essentials.airport: MAF â€” Midland International Air and Space Port (3.5 hours north)

alpine.title: Alpine
alpine.tagline: Cultural Heart of the Big Bend Country
alpine.gettingThere: Drive from Midland (120 miles via US-385 S and TX-118) or El Paso (3 hours via I-10 E and US-90). The Sunset Limited Amtrak train stops in Alpine three times a week â€” the most scenic train ride in Texas. Sul Ross State University is the cultural anchor of this small city of 6,000.
highlights: Museum of the Big Bend at Sul Ross University â€” definitive regional history collection (free)
Marfa (30 minutes west) and its art scene
Big Bend National Park (80 miles south) as basecamp
Railroad Blues bar â€” live music in a converted depot
Reata Restaurant â€” upscale cowboy cuisine in a 100-year-old ranch building
Texas Brewhouse and Spirits local craft beer
Sul Ross State University rodeo arena events
Amtrak Sunset Limited sunset arrival â€” dramatic desert scenery

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential for regional exploration. Alpine itself is walkable downtown. Amtrak for arrival from El Paso or San Antonio. No rideshare.
essentials.airport: MAF â€” Midland International Air and Space Port (2 hours northeast)

del-rio.title: Del Rio
del-rio.tagline: Lake Amistad and the US-Mexico Border
del-rio.gettingThere: Drive from San Antonio (2.5 hours west via US-90) or Eagle Pass (60 miles south via US-277). Del Rio International Airport (DRT) has limited service via American Eagle to Dallas. The city sits on the Rio Grande across from Ciudad AcuÃ±a, Mexico.
highlights: Lake Amistad National Recreation Area â€” 67,000-acre reservoir for boating, fishing, and diving on the Rio Grande ($4/boat)
Seminole Canyon State Park â€” 4,000-year-old Native American pictographs ($5)
Devils River State Natural Area â€” one of the clearest rivers in Texas (permit required, limited access)
Whitehead Memorial Museum â€” Judge Roy Bean and frontier justice history ($5)
Ciudad AcuÃ±a day trip across the border for authentic Mexican dining and shopping
Val Verde Winery â€” Texas's oldest winery, founded 1883, free tastings
Lake Amistad largemouth bass fishing â€” consistently excellent
Pecos River and High Bridge overlook

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Downtown Del Rio walkable near the plaza. No meaningful public transit. Bring US passport for AcuÃ±a crossing.
essentials.airport: DRT â€” Del Rio International Airport (limited) / SAT â€” San Antonio International (2.5 hours east)

eagle-pass.title: Eagle Pass
eagle-pass.tagline: Gateway to Mexico on the Rio Grande
eagle-pass.gettingThere: Drive from San Antonio (2.5 hours southwest via US-57 or US-90). Eagle Pass-Piedras Negras International Airport (ELP) has no commercial service â€” use SAT. Eagle Pass is a major port of entry to Piedras Negras, Coahuila, Mexico.
highlights: Fort Duncan Museum and Park â€” 1849 US Army post on the Rio Grande
Lake Casa Blanca International State Park for bass fishing, camping, and birding ($7)
Piedras Negras, Mexico day trip â€” birthplace of the nacho (1943)
Eagle Pass International Golf Club on the border
Historic downtown plaza and Rio Grande waterfront
Maverick County Historical Museum
Border commerce and authentic Coahuila cuisine in Piedras Negras
Rio Grande birding along the river corridor

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Downtown walkable. International bridge to Piedras Negras on foot. Bring US passport for border crossing.
essentials.airport: SAT â€” San Antonio International Airport (2.5 hours northeast)

laredo.title: Laredo
laredo.tagline: Two Cities on the Rio Grande
laredo.gettingThere: Fly into Laredo International Airport (LRD) â€” American serves LRD with connections through Dallas. Or drive from San Antonio (2.5 hours south via I-35). Laredo is the largest US-Mexico border crossing point by trade volume, across from Nuevo Laredo, Tamaulipas.
highlights: Republic of the Rio Grande Museum â€” 1830s capital building of the short-lived republic ($4)
San Agustin de Laredo Cathedral â€” 1872 Spanish Colonial church on the historic plaza
Lake Casa Blanca International State Park (shared with Eagle Pass area)
Laredo Center for the Arts
Nuevo Laredo, Mexico shopping and dining (bring passport)
Washington's Birthday Celebration â€” month-long festival including an international bridge parade in February
Webb County Heritage Foundation historic downtown tours
World Trade International Bridge â€” busiest port of entry on the US-Mexico border

essentials.gettingAround: Car recommended. Historic downtown El Portal district walkable. El Mercado de San Agustin area for dining. Nuevo Laredo accessible by foot across the international bridge.
essentials.airport: LRD â€” Laredo International Airport

brownsville.title: Brownsville
brownsville.tagline: Southernmost Texas City and Rio Grande Delta
brownsville.gettingThere: Fly into Brownsville/South Padre Island International Airport (BRO) â€” American serves BRO via Dallas. Or fly into Valley International (HRL) in Harlingen (30 min north). Drive from San Antonio (4 hours via US-281 S). Brownsville sits at the southernmost tip of Texas, across from Matamoros, Mexico.
highlights: Gladys Porter Zoo â€” one of the nation's top small zoos with 1,600 animals in open naturalistic habitats ($17)
Palo Alto Battlefield National Historical Park â€” 1846 Mexican-American War site (free)
Historic Brownsville Museum of Fine Art
Matamoros, Mexico day trip across the Gateway International Bridge (passport required)
SpaceX Boca Chica Launch Site â€” Starship test launches visible from public beaches
Boca Chica State Park beach â€” at the mouth of the Rio Grande
Sabal Palm Sanctuary â€” last remnant of the original Rio Grande delta forest
Lower Rio Grande Valley National Wildlife Refuge â€” exceptional tropical birding

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Brownsville Metro bus covers the city ($1). Downtown historic district walkable. International bridge to Matamoros on foot with passport.
essentials.airport: BRO â€” Brownsville/South Padre Island International / HRL â€” Valley International, Harlingen

mcallen.title: McAllen
mcallen.tagline: Rio Grande Valley Shopping and Butterfly Capital
mcallen.gettingThere: Fly into McAllen Miller International Airport (MFE) â€” United and American serve MFE with connections to Houston and Dallas. MFE is 5 miles north of downtown. The Rio Grande Valley's largest city, 4 miles from the Mexican border at Reynosa.
highlights: Quinta Mazatlan â€” urban birding center in a 1930s Spanish Colonial manor ($5)
McAllen Nature Center wildlife reserve (free)
National Butterfly Center â€” 100-acre butterfly and native plant sanctuary ($15)
La Plaza Mall â€” the largest mall in South Texas
Anzalduas County Park on the Rio Grande with excellent birding (free)
Historic McAllen and the La Lomita Chapel in Mission (20 min west)
Reynosa, Mexico shopping and dining day trip via the International Bridge
Hidalgo Pumpkin Festival in October

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. McAllen bus system covers the city. Downtown walkable. Reynosa accessible by international bridge â€” bring passport.
essentials.airport: MFE â€” McAllen Miller International Airport

kingsville.title: Kingsville
kingsville.tagline: Home of the King Ranch â€” World's Largest Ranch
kingsville.gettingThere: Drive from Corpus Christi (40 miles southwest via US-77) or San Antonio (2.5 hours via US-281 and US-77). No commercial airport â€” use CRP. Kingsville is the gateway to the legendary King Ranch.
highlights: King Ranch â€” 825,000-acre working cattle ranch offering tours ($15-45)
King Ranch Museum â€” saddles, carriages, and the history of the world's largest ranch ($5)
King Ranch Saddle Shop â€” authentic hand-tooled leather goods
Texas A&M Kingsville campus â€” the university town feel
Conner Museum of Natural History at TAMUK (free)
Celanese and USS Lexington displays at the South Texas Museum ($6)
Los Laureles Division of King Ranch for wildlife management and hunting
Sarita and Falfurrias small towns on the ranching corridor south

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Downtown Kingsville walkable around the plaza. King Ranch tours depart from the visitor center at the ranch entrance. No public transit.
essentials.airport: CRP â€” Corpus Christi International Airport (40 min northeast)

port-isabel.title: Port Isabel
port-isabel.tagline: Gateway to South Padre Island â€” Shrimping Fleet and Lighthouse
port-isabel.gettingThere: Drive from Harlingen (25 miles via US-77 S and TX-100) or Brownsville (30 miles via TX-48). Fly into Valley International (HRL) or BRO, then drive. Port Isabel sits at the foot of the Queen Isabella Causeway leading to South Padre Island.
highlights: Port Isabel Lighthouse State Historic Site â€” climb for views over the Laguna Madre and South Padre ($4)
Historic downtown Port Isabel seafood restaurants and shrimp fleet
Laguna Madre water sports â€” kayaking, paddleboarding, and windsurfing
Dolphin watching from the causeway banks (free)
South Padre Island accessible across the causeway (free bridge)
Port Isabel Museum ($3)
Fresh shrimp straight off the trawlers at the docks
Fishing in Laguna Madre â€” redfish and trout

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Port Isabel downtown walkable. Free causeway bridge to South Padre Island. No public transit.
essentials.airport: HRL â€” Valley International Airport, Harlingen (25 min northwest)

caddo-lake.title: Caddo Lake
caddo-lake.tagline: Mysterious Cypress Swamp on the Texas-Louisiana Border
caddo-lake.gettingThere: Drive from Marshall (15 miles east via TX-43) or Shreveport, Louisiana (55 miles via US-59 and TX-43). No commercial airport â€” use Shreveport Regional (SHV) or Dallas/Fort Worth (DFW, 3 hours west). Caddo Lake is the only natural lake in Texas.
highlights: Caddo Lake State Park boat tours through the ancient cypress swamp (from $20)
Giant bald cypress trees draped in Spanish moss â€” oldest trees in Texas
Caddo Lake National Wildlife Refuge canoeing and kayaking
Uncertain, Texas â€” the tiny community of 100 people on the lake's shore
Bass and crappie fishing in the bayou channels
Swamp Thing and Ghost Road legends â€” mysterious lights on the lonely county roads
Caddo people cultural history at the local museum
Springtime swamp blooms â€” lotus flowers and iris in the water

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Boat essential for the full Caddo Lake experience â€” rentals available at the state park marina. No public transit.
essentials.airport: SHV â€” Shreveport Regional Airport, Louisiana (55 min east)

jefferson.title: Jefferson
jefferson.tagline: Antebellum River Port Frozen in Time
jefferson.gettingThere: Drive from Dallas (2.5 hours east via I-20 E and TX-59 N) or Shreveport, Louisiana (60 miles west via US-59). No commercial airport nearby â€” use DFW or SHV. Jefferson was the most important city in antebellum Texas, once accessible by steamboat from New Orleans.
highlights: Historic Jefferson downtown â€” entire National Register district of 19th-century commercial buildings
Excelsior House Hotel â€” 1858 hotel that hosted Ulysses Grant and Oscar Wilde
Jefferson General Store and antique shops along Austin Street
Bayou cruises through the cypress swamps by paddleboat ($10)
Jefferson Museum of Telegraphy ($3)
The Grove â€” Jefferson's most famous haunted mansion (tours from $20)
Mardi Gras Jefferson â€” massive February festival with elaborate balls and parade
Caddo Lake (20 miles south) for swamp exploration

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Jefferson's historic district is completely walkable â€” less than 10 blocks. No public transit or rideshare.
essentials.airport: SHV â€” Shreveport Regional Airport, Louisiana (60 min east) / DFW (2.5 hours west)

nacogdoches.title: Nacogdoches
nacogdoches.gettingThere: Drive from Houston (2.5 hours north via US-59) or Dallas (3 hours southeast via US-175). No commercial airport â€” use Houston Bush (IAH) or Dallas/Fort Worth (DFW). Nacogdoches claims to be the oldest continuous human settlement in Texas, with roots to 1716.
highlights: Historic downtown Nacogdoches â€” nine flags have flown over this city
Stephen F. Austin State University campus arboretum (free)
Sterne-Hoya House Museum â€” 1830 home of Nicolas Sterne who baptized Sam Houston ($5)
Old Stone Fort Museum replica â€” original 1779 trading post rebuilt on the SFA campus (free)
Azalea Trails in spring (March-April) through Nacogdoches neighborhoods
The Cookie Bar and Tyler Street antique shops
Lake Nacogdoches fishing and boating
Millard's Crossing Historic Village â€” relocated pioneer cabins ($5)

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Downtown walkable. SFA campus walkable. No public transit or rideshare.
essentials.airport: IAH â€” George Bush Intercontinental, Houston (2.5 hours south)

lufkin.title: Lufkin
lufkin.tagline: Texas Forestry and Angelina National Forest
lufkin.gettingThere: Drive from Houston (2 hours north via US-59) or Dallas (3 hours southeast via US-175 and TX-103). No commercial airport â€” use IAH or DFW. Lufkin is the hub of East Texas Piney Woods and gateway to Angelina National Forest and Sam Rayburn Reservoir.
highlights: Texas Forestry Museum â€” comprehensive history of East Texas timber industry (free)
Museum of East Texas â€” fine art in a converted church ($5)
Angelina National Forest hiking and camping (free with America the Beautiful pass)
Sam Rayburn Reservoir â€” 114,000 acres of bass fishing and lake recreation
Ellen Trout Zoo â€” free municipal zoo with 700 animals
Diboll History Center covering the Long-Bell Lumber Company legacy
SH-94 Scenic Drive through the Piney Woods
Boykin Springs Recreation Area swimming hole in the national forest

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. No public transit. Lufkin is a spread-out East Texas town â€” most attractions require driving between them.
essentials.airport: IAH â€” George Bush Intercontinental, Houston (2 hours south)

tyler.title: Tyler
tyler.gettingThere: Drive from Dallas (1.5 hours east via I-20) or Shreveport (1.5 hours west via I-20). Tyler Pounds Regional Airport (TYR) has limited American Eagle service to Dallas. Car rental at TYR for regional exploration.
highlights: Tyler Rose Garden â€” America's largest municipal rose garden with 38,000 plants of 600+ varieties (free)
Texas Rose Festival in October â€” rose parade, queen's coronation, and garden tours
Caldwell Zoo â€” 85-acre zoo with 4,000 animals ($13)
Tyler Museum of Art ($5)
UT Health East Texas facilities and medical tourism infrastructure
Brookshire's World of Wildlife Museum (free)
Tyler State Park piney woods swimming lake and cabins ($7/person)
The Oil Palace live events venue

essentials.gettingAround: Car recommended. Downtown Tyler and the rose garden are walkable to each other. No meaningful public transit. Uber/Lyft available.
essentials.airport: TYR â€” Tyler Pounds Regional Airport / DFW â€” Dallas/Fort Worth (1.5 hours west)

denton.title: Denton
denton.tagline: Live Music College Town North of Dallas
denton.gettingThere: Drive from Dallas (35 miles north via I-35 E) or Fort Worth (35 miles north via I-35 W). DCTA A-train commuter rail connects Denton to DART light rail in Carrollton â€” access Dallas in 75 minutes ($5). DFW Airport is 30 miles south.
highlights: Denton Square historic courthouse and surrounding bars and restaurants
Rubber Gloves Rehearsal Studios â€” legendary DIY music venue launching Texas bands since 1994
Denton Arts and Jazz Festival in April (free)
UNT and TWU campuses â€” two major universities giving the city its youthful energy
LSA Burger Company for creative Texas burgers
Dan's Silverleaf â€” intimate listening room with eclectic national and local acts
Denton Community Market on Saturday mornings (April-November)
Barrington Square antique mall for vintage Texas finds

essentials.gettingAround: DCTA A-train to Dallas. Downtown Denton walkable. Uber/Lyft available. Car for wider Denton exploration.
essentials.airport: DFW â€” Dallas/Fort Worth International Airport (30 min south)

mckinney.title: McKinney
mckinney.gettingThere: Drive from Dallas (30 miles north via US-75) or DFW Airport (45 miles southwest). No commercial airport at McKinney â€” use DFW or DAL. McKinney National Airport handles private aviation. The historic square is 40 minutes from downtown Dallas on US-75.
highlights: McKinney historic downtown square â€” 100+ locally-owned shops, restaurants, and galleries in 19th-century buildings
Heard Natural Science Museum and Wildlife Sanctuary ($12)
Chestnut Square Historic Village â€” 1850s pioneer structures ($5)
Towne Lake Recreation Area trails and water sports
McKinney Performing Arts Center in the 1875 Collin County Courthouse
Lavender Festival in May at Donahue Farm
The Celt Irish Pub and local craft breweries on the square
Adrian's restaurant for Hill Country-inspired Texas cuisine

essentials.gettingAround: Car recommended. Historic square walkable. No commuter rail â€” DART does not reach McKinney. Uber/Lyft from Dallas.
essentials.airport: DFW â€” Dallas/Fort Worth International Airport (45 min southwest)

grapevine.title: Grapevine
grapevine.tagline: Christmas Capital of Texas and Gateway to DFW
grapevine.gettingThere: TRE commuter rail from DFW Airport Terminal A to Grapevine Main Station (free on the TRE). Or drive from Dallas (30 miles northwest via TX-114) or Fort Worth (20 miles east via TX-114). Grapevine is literally adjacent to DFW Airport â€” many travellers stop for a night.
highlights: Historic Grapevine Main Street â€” boutiques, wineries, and restaurants in turn-of-century buildings
Garvey Vineyard and Delaney Vineyards in Texas's first AVA
Grapevine Vintage Railroad â€” historic steam train excursions ($20-40)
SEA LIFE Grapevine Aquarium at Grapevine Mills ($25)
Legoland Discovery Center at Grapevine Mills ($29)
Grapevine Mills â€” large discount outlet and entertainment mall
GaylordTexas Resort Christmas Wonderland (November-January) â€” 4 million lights and indoor ice skating
Nash Farm Living History Museum ($8)

essentials.gettingAround: TRE train from DFW. Grapevine Main Street walkable. Car for wider exploration. Uber/Lyft active. Dallas Cowboys Shuttle on game days.
essentials.airport: DFW â€” Dallas/Fort Worth International Airport (adjacent â€” free TRE train)

granbury.title: Granbury
granbury.tagline: Best Small Town Square in Texas
granbury.gettingThere: Drive from Fort Worth (35 miles southwest via US-377 or TX-4) or Dallas (60 miles via US-377 S). No commercial airport â€” use DFW or DAL. Granbury sits on Lake Granbury on the Brazos River, 40 miles southwest of Fort Worth.
highlights: Granbury historic town square â€” Texas's first designated Texas Main Street city
Granbury Opera House â€” professional theater since 1886 on the square
Lake Granbury water sports, fishing, and sunset cruises
Acton State Historic Site â€” smallest Texas state park with Elizabeth Crockett's grave ($3)
Granbury Dungeon and Escape Rooms on the square
Harvest Moon CafÃ© and Local Yocal Farm to Market dining
Fort Worth and Wild West history at Granbury museums
Peach Festival in June at local orchards

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Historic square entirely walkable. No public transit. Lake boat rentals for water access.
essentials.airport: DFW â€” Dallas/Fort Worth International Airport (60 min northeast)

glen-rose.title: Glen Rose
glen-rose.tagline: Dinosaur Valley and Fossil Rim Wildlife
glen-rose.gettingThere: Drive from Fort Worth (60 miles southwest via US-67 or Chalk Mountain Road) or Dallas (70 miles via US-67). No commercial airport â€” use DFW or DAL. Glen Rose is famous for having the best-preserved dinosaur trackways in the world, embedded in the Paluxy River.
highlights: Dinosaur Valley State Park â€” walk among 113-million-year-old sauropod and theropod tracks in the riverbed ($7)
Fossil Rim Wildlife Center â€” drive-through African wildlife safari with cheetahs and rhinos on 1,800 Texas acres ($30-75)
Somervell County Museum in historic courthouse ($5)
Creation Evidence Museum for young-Earth interpretation of the fossil site (free)
Paluxy River swimming holes (free, within Dinosaur Valley)
The Promise outdoor drama â€” Christian passion play in a 3,000-seat amphitheater
Comanche Peak Nuclear Power Plant visitor center (tours)
Wildlife feeding stations at Fossil Rim giraffe platform

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Glen Rose itself is tiny and walkable. No public transit. Fossil Rim requires your own vehicle for the drive-through.
essentials.airport: DFW â€” Dallas/Fort Worth International Airport (70 min northeast)

abilene.title: Abilene
abilene.tagline: Frontier Texas Between Dallas and the Big Country
abilene.gettingThere: Fly into Abilene Regional Airport (ABI) â€” American Eagle serves ABI with connections to Dallas. Or drive from Dallas (2.5 hours west via I-20). Abilene is home to three universities (Abilene Christian, Hardin-Simmons, McMurry) giving it an outsized cultural scene for its size.
highlights: National Center for Children's Illustrated Literature (free)
Abilene Zoological Gardens ($10)
Frontier Texas! museum â€” immersive multimedia frontier history experience ($10)
Buffalo Gap Historic Village â€” 30 restored 19th-century buildings ($10)
Lake Abilene State Park hiking and camping ($4)
12th Armored Division Memorial Museum at Dyess AFB (free)
Paramount Theatre â€” 1930 art deco movie palace
Taylor County Exposition Center rodeo and livestock events

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Downtown Abilene walkable around Cypress Street corridor. CityLink bus ($1). Uber/Lyft limited.
essentials.airport: ABI â€” Abilene Regional Airport

canyon.title: Canyon
canyon.gettingThere: Drive from Amarillo (15 miles south via US-87). Canyon is the county seat of Randall County and home to West Texas A&M University. Palo Duro Canyon entrance is 12 miles east via TX-217. Fly into Amarillo (AMA), then drive 15 minutes.
highlights: Palo Duro Canyon State Park â€” Grand Canyon of Texas (12 miles east, $8/person)
TEXAS Outdoor Musical amphitheatre in Palo Duro Canyon (June-August, $20-45)
Panhandle-Plains Historical Museum â€” Texas's largest history museum on the WTAMU campus ($15)
West Texas A&M University campus and the Cornette Library
Pioneer Town replica frontier village in the museum
Canyon's historic downtown square
Palo Duro Creek Brewing Company
Caprock Canyons State Park (40 miles east) for bison viewing

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Canyon's small downtown is walkable. No public transit. Drive to Palo Duro Canyon independently.
essentials.airport: AMA â€” Rick Husband Amarillo International Airport (15 min north)

bryan-college-station.title: Bryan-College Station
bryan-college-station.tagline: Home of the Aggies â€” Gig 'Em!
bryan-college-station.gettingThere: Drive from Houston (1.5 hours via TX-6 N) or Austin (1.5 hours via TX-21 and TX-6). Easterwood Airport (CLL) in College Station has American Eagle service to Dallas. The twin cities sit in the Brazos Valley, 90 miles north of Houston.
highlights: Texas A&M University campus â€” 5,200-acre campus including Kyle Field (102,000-seat stadium)
George H.W. Bush Presidential Library and Museum ($10)
Aggieland on game days â€” legendary 12th Man crowd energy
Messina Hof Winery and Resort in Bryan (free tour with tasting)
Research Valley Innovation Corridor â€” NASA research facilities nearby
Traditions at Texas A&M â€” Midnight Yell Practice and Muster ceremony
Downtown Bryan Arts District revitalization
Brazos Valley Museum of Natural History ($5)

essentials.gettingAround: Brazos Transit bus covers both cities ($1.50). Texas A&M campus walkable. Car recommended for wider exploration. Uber/Lyft available.
essentials.airport: CLL â€” Easterwood Airport, College Station

temple-belton.title: Temple-Belton
temple-belton.tagline: Central Texas Lakes and the I-35 Corridor
temple-belton.gettingThere: Drive from Austin (1 hour north via I-35) or Dallas (2 hours south via I-35). The twin cities sit at the crossroads of I-35 and I-14 (the Central Texas Expressway). Fort Hood (now Fort Cavazos) is adjacent â€” one of the world's largest military installations.
highlights: Lake Belton â€” 12,300-acre reservoir for boating, bass fishing, and waterfront camping
Lake Stillhouse Hollow â€” adjacent reservoir with canoeing and hiking
Bell County Museum in Belton â€” Central Texas history ($5)
Temple Railroad and Heritage Museum (free)
Sammons Golf Course on Lake Belton
Topsey Exotic Ranch wildlife encounters near Copperas Cove
Main Street Temple arts district and local restaurants
Fort Cavazos (formerly Fort Hood) 1st Cavalry Division Museum (free, base access required)

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. No meaningful public transit. I-35 connects to Austin and Dallas. Uber/Lyft limited outside the city core.
essentials.airport: AUS â€” Austin-Bergstrom International Airport (60 min south)

killeen.title: Killeen
killeen.tagline: Home of Fort Cavazos and Central Texas Military Community
killeen.gettingThere: Drive from Austin (1 hour north via I-35 and TX-195) or Waco (1 hour south via I-35 to TX-195). Killeen-Fort Hood Regional Airport (GRK) has American Eagle service to Dallas. Adjacent to Fort Cavazos (formerly Fort Hood) â€” one of the largest US Army bases.
highlights: 1st Cavalry Division Museum on Fort Cavazos (free with base access)
Killeen Civic and Conference Center events
Stillhouse Hollow Lake recreation area for fishing and camping
Lion's Junction Family Water Park (summer, $15)
Central Texas State Veterans Cemetery (free)
Texas Ranger Outpost Museum ($5)
Chain restaurant corridor on Fort Hood Boulevard
Copperas Cove Market Days (monthly, free)

essentials.gettingAround: Car essential. Military community â€” ID may be required for some base facilities. Killeen Transit bus ($1.25). Uber/Lyft available.
essentials.airport: GRK â€” Killeen-Fort Hood Regional Airport

country.name: Texas, USA
country.mainAirport: Dallas/Fort Worth International (DFW) â€” primary hub. Austin-Bergstrom (AUS), Houston Bush (IAH), and San Antonio (SAT) also major gateways.
country.language: English (primary), Spanish (widely spoken throughout the state)
country.timezone: Central Time (CT) â€” UTC-6 standard, UTC-5 daylight. El Paso and far West Texas: Mountain Time (MT).
country.visaInfo: Texas is part of the United States. Visitors from Visa Waiver Program countries use ESTA (apply online, $21, valid 2 years). Canadian citizens do not need a visa. Others require a US tourist visa (B-2).
country.electricalPlug: Type A and B (US standard flat two/three-pin)
country.bestOverallMonths: March-April (spring wildflowers and mild weather) and October-November (comfortable temperatures, fall foliage in East Texas). Avoid June-August in most of Texas â€” extreme heat with temperatures regularly exceeding 100Â°F (38Â°C).
country.gettingAround: Car is essential for virtually all Texas travel. The state is larger than France. Rideshare (Uber/Lyft) covers major cities. Limited Amtrak service (Sunset Limited, Texas Eagle). No intercity bus network comparable to other states.
country.tipping: 15-20% at restaurants is standard and expected. $1-2/drink at bars. $2-5/bag for hotel bellhops.
country.foodBudget: Food trucks and taquerias $5-12. BBQ joints $15-25/plate. Mid-range restaurants $20-50/person. Fine dining $80-150+ per person.
country.stateParks: Texas State Parks entry is $2-8/person. Annual pass ($70) covers 90+ parks. Reserve campsites and day passes at texasstateparks.reserveamerica.com â€” popular parks sell out weeks in advance.
